groups.title: Essentials
groups.description: Announcement type, company, news summary, audience, facts, date, dateline, goal, length, language, and geographic scope.

groups.title: Message & Evidence
groups.description: Benefits, supporting evidence, references, timing, reader action, sensitive claims, and background context.

groups.title: Quotes & Company
groups.description: Quote controls, authorized speakers, approved quotation material, boilerplate, website, media contact, and brand voice.

groups.title: Distribution Settings
groups.description: Publication destinations, editorial status, headline style, subheadline, and notes-to-editors controls.

groups.title: Compliance & Final Details
groups.description: Release or embargo status, embargo date, and additional requirements.

variables.label: Announcement type
variables.placeholder: Select the type of announcement
variables.hint: Helps structure the release around the correct kind of news.

help.what: Provide the announcement type for this press release.
help.why: Different announcement types require different leads, evidence, chronology, and emphasis.
help.example: Good example: Product or service launch
help.avoid: Avoid vague placeholders, missing facts, unsupported claims, or information that is not approved for public use.

variables.label: Company or organization
variables.placeholder: Example: Northstar Analytics
variables.hint: Enter the official name that should appear in the release.

help.what: Provide the company or organization for this press release.
help.why: The issuing company or organization cannot be safely inferred.
help.example: Good example: Northstar Analytics
help.avoid: Avoid vague placeholders, missing facts, unsupported claims, or information that is not approved for public use.

variables.label: What are you announcing?
variables.placeholder: Describe the announcement, what is changing, and why it matters.
variables.hint: Include the central news in plain language, not a polished press release.

help.what: Provide the what are you announcing? for this press release.
help.why: This provides the central news angle and determines the release’s lead.
help.example: Good example: Northstar Analytics is launching a workflow dashboard for regional logistics teams.
help.avoid: Avoid turning this into final copy, adding claims not approved for publication, or burying the actual announcement.

variables.label: Primary audience
variables.placeholder: Example: technology journalists, small-business owners, and industry analysts
variables.hint: Identify the journalists, readers, customers, or stakeholders the news should reach.

help.what: Provide the primary audience for this press release.
help.why: Audience information materially changes terminology, emphasis, context, and news value.
help.example: Good example: Technology journalists, logistics operators, and regional business publications
help.avoid: Avoid vague placeholders, missing facts, unsupported claims, or information that is not approved for public use.

variables.label: Essential facts
variables.placeholder: List verified dates, names, features, amounts, locations, results, and other required facts.
variables.hint: Provide only facts that may be stated publicly.

help.what: Provide the essential facts for this press release.
help.why: A press release must be grounded in supplied, verifiable information.
help.example: Good example: Launch date: September 12, 2026. Available in the UK and EU. Integrates with existing delivery-management tools.
help.avoid: Avoid unverified numbers, invented dates, unsupported names, vague superlatives, or facts that are not public.

variables.label: Release date
variables.placeholder: Select the publication date
variables.hint: Used in the dateline and to interpret whether the announcement is upcoming or completed.

help.what: Provide the release date for this press release.
help.why: The publication date affects tense, timing language, and dateline accuracy.
help.example: Good example: 2026-09-12
help.avoid: Avoid guessed dates, cities, countries, time zones, or locations chosen only for prestige.

variables.label: Dateline location
variables.placeholder: Example: Berlin, Germany
variables.hint: Enter the city and country or region associated with the announcement.

help.what: Provide the dateline location for this press release.
help.why: A conventional press release normally requires an accurate dateline location.
help.example: Good example: Berlin, Germany
help.avoid: Avoid guessed dates, cities, countries, time zones, or locations chosen only for prestige.

variables.label: Primary goal
variables.placeholder: Select the main goal
variables.hint: Guides the news angle and final reader action without making the release overly promotional.

help.what: Optional context for the primary goal field.
help.why: The desired outcome determines which facts and implications deserve the strongest emphasis.
help.example: Good example: Earn media coverage
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, promotional exaggeration, fake urgency, fake scarcity, unsupported superlatives, private data, or unapproved claims.

variables.label: Release length
variables.placeholder: Select a preferred length
variables.hint: Choose a useful editorial range rather than an exact word count.

help.what: Optional context for the release length field.
help.why: The appropriate depth depends on the complexity and importance of the announcement.
help.example: Good example: Standard
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, promotional exaggeration, fake urgency, fake scarcity, unsupported superlatives, private data, or unapproved claims.

variables.label: Output language
variables.placeholder: Select the release language
variables.hint: The release will use native professional conventions for the selected language.

help.what: Optional context for the output language field.
help.why: Language affects grammar, dateline conventions, quotation punctuation, and editorial tone.
help.example: Good example: English
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, promotional exaggeration, fake urgency, fake scarcity, unsupported superlatives, private data, or unapproved claims.

variables.label: Geographic scope
variables.placeholder: Example: United Kingdom, European Union, global, or local market
variables.hint: Clarify where the announcement, product, event, or service applies.

help.what: Optional context for the geographic scope field.
help.why: Geographic scope prevents the release from implying availability or relevance in unsupported markets.
help.example: Good example: United Kingdom and European Union
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, promotional exaggeration, fake urgency, fake scarcity, unsupported superlatives, private data, or unapproved claims.

variables.label: Main significance or benefits
variables.placeholder: Explain who benefits, what improves, and why the announcement matters.
variables.hint: Use specific outcomes and significance rather than advertising slogans.

help.what: Optional context for the main significance or benefits field.
help.why: This helps translate raw news into a relevant story for journalists and readers.
help.example: Good example: Helps operations teams see route delays, handoff issues, and late deliveries in one dashboard.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, promotional exaggeration, fake urgency, fake scarcity, unsupported superlatives, private data, or unapproved claims.

variables.label: Evidence and measurable details
variables.placeholder: Add verified statistics, research findings, milestones, comparisons, or performance data.
variables.hint: Include methodology, dates, sample sizes, or attribution when relevant.

help.what: Optional context for the evidence and measurable details field.
help.why: Evidence can strengthen newsworthiness but must come directly from the user.
help.example: Good example: Pilot customers reported identifying recurring delay patterns during a 60-day internal trial.
help.avoid: Avoid unsupported statistics, missing methodology, unclear sample sizes, or comparisons without a basis.

variables.label: Sources and reference material
variables.placeholder: Paste source links, document titles, approved background notes, or factual references.
variables.hint: References guide verification but will not be invented or described as independently checked.

help.what: Optional context for the sources and reference material field.
help.why: References help attribute claims and identify facts that require confirmation.
help.example: Good example: Internal launch brief, approved product FAQ, and September 2026 availability note.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, promotional exaggeration, fake urgency, fake scarcity, unsupported superlatives, private data, or unapproved claims.

variables.label: Timing and availability
variables.placeholder: Add launch dates, event dates, rollout stages, availability, deadlines, or relevant timing.
variables.hint: Only include dates and availability details that are confirmed.

help.what: Optional context for the timing and availability field.
help.why: Press releases frequently depend on precise launch, availability, or event timing.
help.example: Good example: Public launch begins September 12, 2026, after a private beta with selected logistics partners.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, promotional exaggeration, fake urgency, fake scarcity, unsupported superlatives, private data, or unapproved claims.

variables.label: Preferred reader action
variables.placeholder: Example: visit the newsroom, register, download the report, or request an interview
variables.hint: Leave as Auto for one restrained, context-appropriate next step.

help.what: Optional context for the preferred reader action field.
help.why: It determines the release’s single proportional CTA without adding competing actions.
help.example: Good example: Visit the newsroom or request a product briefing
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, promotional exaggeration, fake urgency, fake scarcity, unsupported superlatives, private data, or unapproved claims.

variables.label: Claims or wording to avoid
variables.placeholder: List unapproved claims, confidential topics, legal restrictions, or prohibited wording.
variables.hint: Use this for specific restrictions, not general writing instructions.

help.what: Optional context for the claims or wording to avoid field.
help.why: Company-specific legal, regulatory, contractual, or confidentiality restrictions cannot be inferred.
help.example: Good example: Do not claim guaranteed delivery improvements or regulatory compliance.
help.avoid: Avoid general style preferences when the field should capture concrete prohibited claims or legal boundaries.

variables.label: Background and context
variables.placeholder: Add relevant history, earlier announcements, market context, or necessary explanation.
variables.hint: Include only context that helps readers understand the current news.

help.what: Optional context for the background and context field.
help.why: Relevant background can make unfamiliar or technical announcements understandable.
help.example: Good example: The dashboard follows the company’s earlier route-monitoring API release.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, promotional exaggeration, fake urgency, fake scarcity, unsupported superlatives, private data, or unapproved claims.

variables.label: Include quotations
variables.placeholder: Select whether to include quotations
variables.hint: Turn on only when an authorized speaker and approved quotation material are available.

help.what: Optional context for the include quotations field.
help.why: Quotations are common in press releases but should not be invented automatically.
help.example: Good example: On
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, promotional exaggeration, fake urgency, fake scarcity, unsupported superlatives, private data, or unapproved claims.

variables.label: Authorized speakers
variables.placeholder: List each speaker’s full name, title, organization, and relevance to the announcement.
variables.hint: Only include people authorized to be quoted publicly.

help.what: Optional context for the authorized speakers field.
help.why: The model must know exactly who is authorized to speak and how they should be identified.
help.example: Good example: Ava Brooks, CEO of Northstar Analytics, authorized to comment on product strategy.
help.avoid: Avoid invented quotations, unapproved speakers, altered meanings, fake titles, or statements presented as approved when they are only draft ideas.

variables.label: Approved quotation material
variables.placeholder: Paste approved quotes or exact approved ideas that may be turned into a draft for review.
variables.hint: Unapproved draft wording will be clearly marked outside the publishable release.

help.what: Optional context for the approved quotation material field.
help.why: It prevents fabricated quotations and distinguishes approved wording from suggested language.
help.example: Good example: This launch gives regional teams a clearer way to understand where delivery workflows slow down.
help.avoid: Avoid invented quotations, unapproved speakers, altered meanings, fake titles, or statements presented as approved when they are only draft ideas.

variables.label: Company boilerplate
variables.placeholder: Paste the approved “About the company” paragraph or provide verified company facts.
variables.hint: If omitted, use a minimal factual paragraph or a completion placeholder.

help.what: Optional context for the company boilerplate field.
help.why: The boilerplate contains company facts that should not be inferred or fabricated.
help.example: Good example: Northstar Analytics builds workflow visibility tools for regional logistics teams.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, promotional exaggeration, fake urgency, fake scarcity, unsupported superlatives, private data, or unapproved claims.

variables.label: Company website
variables.placeholder: https://example.com
variables.hint: Used only as a factual destination when a website link is appropriate.

help.what: Optional context for the company website field.
help.why: The official website cannot be safely invented.
help.example: Good example: https://example.com
help.avoid: Avoid invented URLs, tracking-modified links, unrelated pages, or links that are not approved for publication.

variables.label: Media contact
variables.placeholder: Add contact name, role, email address, phone number, or media inquiry URL.
variables.hint: Provide only business contact details approved for public publication.

help.what: Optional context for the media contact field.
help.why: Journalists require a valid contact, but private or guessed information must never be inserted.
help.example: Good example: Media Relations, press@example.com, +44 20 0000 0000
help.avoid: Avoid private personal details, guessed email formats, personal social profiles, or contact information not approved for publication.

variables.label: Brand voice
variables.placeholder: Select the preferred voice
variables.hint: The release will remain journalistic and restrained regardless of the selected voice.

help.what: Optional context for the brand voice field.
help.why: Brand voice affects diction and tone without changing factual standards.
help.example: Good example: Clear and professional
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, promotional exaggeration, fake urgency, fake scarcity, unsupported superlatives, private data, or unapproved claims.

variables.label: Publication destinations
variables.placeholder: Select intended destinations
variables.hint: The release will be formatted for the selected distribution environments.

help.what: Optional context for the publication destinations field.
help.why: Newswire, newsroom, trade, local, and stakeholder releases require different levels of context and formality.
help.example: Good example: Company newsroom, Media outreach
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, promotional exaggeration, fake urgency, fake scarcity, unsupported superlatives, private data, or unapproved claims.

variables.label: Editorial status
variables.placeholder: Select the current status
variables.hint: Controls whether incomplete details appear as review notes or clearly marked placeholders.

help.what: Optional context for the editorial status field.
help.why: The treatment of missing information should differ between an early draft and final publication copy.
help.example: Good example: Draft for review
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, promotional exaggeration, fake urgency, fake scarcity, unsupported superlatives, private data, or unapproved claims.

variables.label: Headline style
variables.placeholder: Select a headline approach
variables.hint: Headlines remain factual and avoid clickbait regardless of style.

help.what: Optional context for the headline style field.
help.why: The preferred angle changes how the same announcement is framed for editors.
help.example: Good example: Straight news
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, promotional exaggeration, fake urgency, fake scarcity, unsupported superlatives, private data, or unapproved claims.

variables.label: Include a subheadline
variables.placeholder: Select whether to include a subheadline
variables.hint: A subheadline will be included only when it adds useful context.

help.what: Optional context for the include a subheadline field.
help.why: Some newsroom and wire formats benefit from a supporting line, while others require a leaner presentation.
help.example: Good example: On
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, promotional exaggeration, fake urgency, fake scarcity, unsupported superlatives, private data, or unapproved claims.

variables.label: Include notes to editors
variables.placeholder: Select whether to include notes to editors
variables.hint: Use for background details, asset availability, interview access, or editorial clarification.

help.what: Optional context for the include notes to editors field.
help.why: Notes to editors are useful for media distribution but unnecessary for many public newsroom releases.
help.example: Good example: On
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, promotional exaggeration, fake urgency, fake scarcity, unsupported superlatives, private data, or unapproved claims.

variables.label: Notes to editors content
variables.placeholder: Add media assets, interview availability, background notes, pronunciations, or access details.
variables.hint: Keep information factual and separate from the public release body.

help.what: Optional context for the notes to editors content field.
help.why: The model cannot infer which additional resources or media arrangements are available.
help.example: Good example: Product screenshots and executive interviews are available by request.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, promotional exaggeration, fake urgency, fake scarcity, unsupported superlatives, private data, or unapproved claims.

variables.label: Embargo status
variables.placeholder: Select release timing
variables.hint: Do not select an embargo unless recipients have agreed to respect it.

help.what: Optional context for the embargo status field.
help.why: Release timing changes the status line and may create a significant distribution restriction.
help.example: Good example: Embargoed
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, promotional exaggeration, fake urgency, fake scarcity, unsupported superlatives, private data, or unapproved claims.

options: Do not display a status line

variables.label: Embargo date
variables.placeholder: Select the embargo end date
variables.hint: The exact time and time zone may be added in Additional Requirements.

help.what: Optional context for the embargo date field.
help.why: An embargo date must be supplied explicitly and must never be guessed.
help.example: Good example: 2026-09-10
help.avoid: Avoid approximate dates, missing time-zone details, or embargoes that recipients have not agreed to respect.

variables.label: Additional requirements
variables.placeholder: Add jurisdiction, time zone, required wording, formatting rules, or other final instructions.
variables.hint: Use only for important details not covered elsewhere.

help.what: Optional context for the additional requirements field.
help.why: It provides one controlled place for legitimate requirements that do not justify separate fields.
help.example: Good example: Embargo lifts at 09:00 CET. Use UK English and avoid unsupported market-size claims.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, promotional exaggeration, fake urgency, fake scarcity, unsupported superlatives, private data, or unapproved claims.
